title: The Sege of Thebes and The Sege of Troye
summary: CME00076 is the repository XML for a University of Michigan manually keyed and proofread encoding of the Middle English text on Friedrich Brie's 1913 journal pages 47–52 and 269–285. Brie printed two distinct anonymous prose romances from one working transcript made by Fritz Jung from Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS. Rawl. D. 82: The Sege of Thebes, now cataloged at inferred fols. 1r–10v, and The Sege of Troye, at inferred fols. 11r–24v. Brie argues, and current scholarship confirms, that the two prose works abridge John Lydgate's Siege of Thebes and Troy Book respectively. A complete scan of Archiv volume 130 and a complete PMLA issue containing Nathaniel E. Griffin's independent 1907 Troy edition are cached locally. No complete public facsimile of MS. Rawl. D. 82 was found: Late Medieval English Scribes exposes selected images of fols. 1r and 25r, but only fol. 1r overlaps CME00076, while the Bodleian offers mediated scans and high-resolution photography by request. Any such request should cover the complete 34-leaf codex, six later endleaves, and binding surfaces, then map the corpus works separately to fols. 1r–24v.
review_notes: CME00076 is an editorial composite of two distinct medieval works, not one medieval romance. Part-level scopes prevent the Thebes and Troy subjects, sources, and loci from being conflated.
The XML omits Brie's German source study on pp. 40–46 but retains the transcription statement and both complete Middle English text runs: Thebes on pp. 47–52 and 269–272, and Troy on pp. 273–285.
The unnumbered PB element between printed pp. 52 and 270 represents p. 269, the opening page of the second journal installment.

Griffin printed The Sege of Troye in 1907, six years before Brie described both prose romances as previously unknown and their texts as first reproduced. Griffin is therefore a prior independent edition, not Brie's source and not an ancestor of the CME encoding.

Griffin proposed an unidentified French intermediary for Troy. Brie later demonstrated dependence on Lydgate's Troy Book, and the current METS Troy Book introduction identifies the prose Sege of Troy as an early-to-mid-fifteenth-century derivative of Lydgate.

The Bodleian's 2025 description calls the part-1 loci inferred and says that part has no foliation; Late Medieval English Scribes instead reports modern pencil foliation 1–34. The work ranges are usable institutional locators but should not be represented as an unproblematic medieval foliation.

The complete-source rule applies to the physical manuscript, not merely to the 24 leaves containing the corpus item. A future complete surrogate should retain adjacent Gower material, later verse, blanks, endleaves, pastedowns, covers or boards, spine, and edges when offered.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00076
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow and supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00076
entities.description: A public encoding of Brie's two Middle English prose texts, German transcription headnote, footnotes, printed-page breaks, manuscript-folio milestones, and italicized expansions. It excludes Brie's pp. 40–46 introduction and source comparison.

entities.label: Anonymous prose Sege of Thebes and prose Sege of Troye
entities.description: A conceptual grouping of two distinct anonymous Middle English prose romances copied together by one hand. Thebes abridges Lydgate's Siege of Thebes; Troy abridges Lydgate's Troy Book. This entity does not merge the two works or stand for the physical codex.

date_statements.label: Composition of both prose adaptations
date_statements.value: After 1422 and probably by about the middle of the fifteenth century

entities.label: Friedrich Brie, Zwei mittelenglische Prosaromane (1913)
alternate_labels: Zwei mittelenglische Prosaromane: The Sege of Thebes und The Sege of Troy (1422 bis ca. 1450)
entities.description: The immediate printed source of CME00076. Brie's article introduces the two romances on pp. 40–46, prints Thebes on pp. 47–52 and 269–272, and prints Troy on pp. 273–285. Brie credits Fritz Jung for the transcript, expands manuscript abbreviations in italics, and supplies selected Lydgate parallels in footnotes.

bibliographic.citation: Friedrich Brie, 'Zwei mittelenglische Prosaromane: The Sege of Thebes und The Sege of Troy (1422 bis ca. 1450),' Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen 130 (1913), 40–52, 269–285.
bibliographic.title: Zwei mittelenglische Prosaromane: The Sege of Thebes und The Sege of Troy
bibliographic.container_title: Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen

physical_description.extent: Article pp. 40–52 and 269–285; encoded Middle English text pp. 47–52 and 269–285
physical_description.format: Journal article and diplomatic single-manuscript edition
notes: The article continues in a later portion of the same journal volume.
The title-page subtitle dates the prose works 1422 to circa 1450.

entities.label: Fritz Jung's working transcript of MS. Rawl. D. 82, fols. 1r–24v
entities.description: The material intermediary from which Brie printed the two prose romances. Brie explicitly thanks Dr. Fritz Jung of Pforzheim for the transcript. It is provisionally typed catalog_description because schema 1.0.0 has no dedicated working-transcript type; it is not a second medieval witness.

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS. Rawl. D. 82
entities.description: A fifteenth-century English paper codex of 34 leaves with three later endleaves at each end. Part 1, leaves 1–24, contains the two prose romances in one hybrida hand; part 2 contains a Gower extract and later verse. The current catalog infers Thebes at fols. 1r–10v and Troy at fols. 11r–24v.

physical_description.extent: iii later endleaves + 34 manuscript leaves + iii later endleaves
physical_description.format: Composite codex in a non-contemporary binding repaired in 1986
notes: Part 1 comprises three eight-leaf quarto quires and has 29 ruled and written lines in one hybrida hand.
Thebes is cataloged at inferred fols. 1r–10v; Troy at inferred fols. 11r–24v.
Part 2, fols. 25–34, contains a Confessio Amantis extract and later verse and is outside the CME00076 work boundary but inside the complete physical object.
The current catalog says part 1 has no foliation, while Late Medieval English Scribes reports modern pencil foliation 1–34.

entities.label: John Lydgate's Siege of Thebes
entities.description: The circa-1421–1422 Middle English verse work abridged and recast in prose as The Sege of Thebes. This abstract source-work entity does not identify the prose adapter's physical exemplar.

entities.label: John Lydgate's Troy Book
entities.description: The Middle English verse work composed 1412–1420 and abridged in prose as The Sege of Troye. The source-work entity is distinct from Guido delle Colonne's Latin source and from any one surviving Troy Book manuscript.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Archiv volume 130 (1913)
entities.description: A complete 488-page PDF of the bound journal volume containing both installments of Brie's article. It is a print-volume facsimile, not a manuscript facsimile.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete PDF scan of printed journal volume

entities.label: Nathaniel E. Griffin, The Sege of Troye (1907)
entities.description: An independent prior edition and source study of the Troy work, printed directly from MS. Rawl. D. 82. Griffin normalizes capitals, paragraphing, punctuation, and compound word division while italicizing expanded contractions. He proposed an unidentified French intermediary behind the prose text; this source theory was superseded by Brie's Lydgate analysis and current scholarship.

bibliographic.citation: Nathaniel E. Griffin, 'The Sege of Troye,' Publications of the Modern Language Association of America 22, no. 1 (1907), 157–200, https://doi.org/10.2307/456664.
bibliographic.title: The Sege of Troye
bibliographic.container_title: Publications of the Modern Language Association of America

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of PMLA 22.1 (1907)
entities.description: A complete issue-level PDF containing Griffin's article on printed pp. 157–200. It is a printed-edition scan, not a manuscript facsimile.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete PDF scan of printed journal issue

entities.label: Late Medieval English Scribes selected image of MS. Rawl. D. 82, fol. 1r
entities.description: A single color Zoomify page view of the opening leaf of The Sege of Thebes. It covers neither the whole work nor the complete manuscript and carries an all-rights-reserved/private-study-only notice.

entities.label: Late Medieval English Scribes selected image of MS. Rawl. D. 82, fol. 25r
entities.description: A single color Zoomify page view sampled for Hand 2. Fol. 25r follows the Troy text's inferred ending at fol. 24v, so this supporting image has zero overlap with CME00076 and is not a complete manuscript facsimile.

agents.name: Fritz Jung
notes: Brie identifies him only as Dr. Fritz Jung of Pforzheim and credits him with the manuscript transcript.

Two distinct prose works copied consecutively

scope.notes: The image has zero overlap with CME00076, whose inferred manuscript locus ends at fol. 24v.

notes: The cached PDF covers the whole bound journal volume; the corpus work occupies two non-contiguous article runs inside it.

access.format: HTML catalog record and TEI source

local_copies.label: Bodleian catalog TEI for MS. Rawl. D. 82

notes: Most cataloged manuscripts may be ordered to a Weston Library reading room after registration; item-specific conservation restrictions can still apply.

access.format: View-only Zoomify image of one leaf
notes: Coverage is partial: fol. 1r is the only LMES image that overlaps CME00076. It does not cover Troy, the complete Thebes work, or the whole manuscript.
The provider permits private study but requires written permission for other use; no local copy was made.

access.format: View-only Zoomify image of one leaf
notes: Coverage is partial and has zero overlap with CME00076: fol. 25r follows the Troy work's inferred ending at fol. 24v.
The provider permits private study but requires written permission for other use; no local copy was made.

The exact catalog TEI contains no digital-facsimile link, both tested catalog ARKs do not resolve to Digital Bodleian image objects, and the only verified manuscript image routes are the two LMES sample leaves, fols. 1r and 25r.
Only fol. 1r overlaps CME00076; neither selected image establishes complete work or manuscript digitization.
This dated result does not prove that the manuscript has never been photographed or that an internal surrogate does not exist.

access.format: Reference-quality PDF scans supplied by file transfer
notes: The service is available to all, ordinarily charges non-Oxford requesters £1 per scan with a £10 minimum, and assesses conservation and legal suitability case by case.
Completed forms and questions may be emailed to mediated.copying@bodleian.ox.ac.uk.

For complete-source coverage request the entire physical object: front and rear covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, all three front and three rear later endleaves including blanks, every recto and verso of manuscript leaves 1–34, and edges when offered. State separately that CME00076 occupies Thebes 1r–10v and Troy 11r–24v.
Reference scans are not automatically authorized for repository redistribution or commercial print-on-demand use.
The posted rate is £27 for the first image, £7.50 for images 2–99, and £6.15 for subsequent images 100+, with a £20 minimum; large orders are negotiated.
Use the same complete-object surface list as for mediated copying and ask for a quote before assuming per-image pricing for the whole codex.
Separate permission is required for commercial and many non-academic uses.

local_copies.label: Bodleian Imaging Services terms of use

notes: Current scholarly introduction and digital edition of the source work; not a facsimile of Rawl. D. 82.
Current scholarly introduction and selections, with a public EEBO-TCP transcription of the 1513 print as an alternate source-work representation; neither is a facsimile of Rawl. D. 82.

rights.basis: The anonymous texts and their Lydgate sources are medieval. This does not determine rights in modern transcriptions, editorial apparatus, or photographs.

rights.component: University of Michigan CME digital text
rights.basis: The CME collection states that its materials are in the public domain in the United States and applies the No Copyright–United States rights statement.
The edition was published in 1913; U-M selected it for a collection asserted No Copyright–United States, and Internet Archive marks the scanned volume NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT.
The article was published in the United States in 1907 and the complete issue is publicly downloadable. This statement does not grant rights in Cambridge Core interface assets or modern metadata.
Internet Archive metadata labels the item NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT but supplies no separate open-image license.

rights.component: Internet Archive complete-issue PDF scan and access layer
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and applicable copyright law
rights.basis: The underlying 1907 issue is public domain in the United States, but no item-level open license or provider rights assertion was verified for this exact scan derivative.

rights.component: Selected manuscript photograph of fol. 1r and Zoomify derivatives
rights.basis: Late Medieval English Scribes states that the images remain copyrighted by the holding library and prohibits use beyond private study without written permission.

rights.component: Selected manuscript photograph of fol. 25r and Zoomify derivatives
rights.basis: Late Medieval English Scribes states that the images remain copyrighted by the holding library and prohibits use beyond private study without written permission.

rights.component: Future reference-quality scans supplied through Bodleian mediated copying
rights.basis: The mediated-copying form states that scans of out-of-copyright material are © Bodleian Libraries and supplied under CC BY-NC 4.0; fuller terms prohibit commercial reuse without permission. No item-specific delivery terms have yet been received.

rights.component: Future high-resolution photographs supplied through Bodleian Imaging Services
rights.basis: The Imaging Services order form states that images of out-of-copyright material are © Bodleian Libraries and supplied under CC BY-NC 4.0; the fuller terms prohibit commercial reuse without permission. No item-specific delivery terms have yet been received.
Local caching documents research access and does not establish permission to redistribute scans, manuscript photographs, or provider forms through Git or publication packages.

editorial_practices.description: Brie says the text is otherwise printed without alteration and that manuscript abbreviations alone are expanded and identified by italic type.
editorial_practices.scope: Both edited prose romances, pp. 47–52 and 269–285

editorial_practices.representation: Selected Lydgate line references in footnotes
editorial_practices.description: Brie's notes point to selected corresponding passages in Lydgate; these are source parallels, not readings collated from a second witness of the prose works.
editorial_practices.scope: Both texts and the pp. 40–46 source study

editorial_practices.representation: Transcription headnote and both Middle English texts retained; pp. 40–46 German introduction omitted
editorial_practices.description: The encoding is complete for the two edited Middle English works but selective relative to the full scholarly article.
The XML preserves printed italic spans but does not encode the manuscript abbreviation signs that they replace. A manuscript image is therefore required to study the original shapes.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Split serial pagination and manuscript folio changes
editorial_practices.representation: 23 PB elements and 45 folio MILESTONE elements
editorial_practices.description: Thebes runs from pp. 47–52 to p. 269 and continues through p. 272; the p. 269 PB has no N value. Folio milestones mark changes after the opening rectos and should not be mistaken for a complete foliation inventory.

editorial_practices.representation: Normalized capitalization; editorial paragraphing and punctuation; joined compound elements; contractions expanded in italics
editorial_practices.description: Griffin explicitly describes these interventions and also notes unreproduced strokes and flourishes. His typography is not a facsimile of the manuscript.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00076.xml at Kyvero-Press/CME commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: TEMPHEAD; IDG; TEXT/@LANG; BODY/DIV1[1–2]; PB, MILESTONE, NOTE, HI, HEAD, CLOSER, and SIGNED
evidence.summary: Direct evidence for identifiers, two-part structure, English and German content, printed and manuscript locators, Brie's transcription credit and editorial statement, both prose texts, notes, closers, and source-editor signature.

evidence.citation: Reproducible XML structure and page-sequence audit of CME00076.xml.
evidence.summary: Counts two DIV1 prose-romance containers, five P elements including the German headnote, 23 PB, 45 folio MILESTONE, 37 NOTE, 758 italic HI, two HEAD, two CLOSER, and one SIGNED. Thebes has page sequence 47–52, unnumbered 269, 270–272 and 19 folio milestones; Troy has pp. 273–285 and 26 folio milestones.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection statement.
evidence.summary: States that the collection was manually keyed from public-domain editions, proofread in house, made publicly and bulk-downloadably available, and asserted No Copyright–United States.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan CME file inventory with links, CME00076.
evidence.summary: Identifies CME00076 as Brie's Zwei mittelenglische Prosaromane from Archiv 130 (1913) and links the item and XML family.

evidence.citation: Friedrich Brie, 'Zwei mittelenglische Prosaromane: The Sege of Thebes und The Sege of Troy,' Archiv 130 (1913), 40–52 and 269–285, complete Internet Archive volume scan.
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for edition bibliography, two works, one-author argument, 1422–c.1450 dating, Rawl. D. 82 source, Jung transcript, Lydgate derivations, abbreviation-expansion practice, footnote method, and complete edited texts.

evidence.citation: Page-by-page inspection of Brie's article in the cached Archiv volume 130 PDF.
evidence.summary: Confirms the split article, edition boundaries, p. 269 continuation, transition from Thebes to Troy on p. 273, printed italics and footnotes, and Brie's final Freiburg signature on p. 285.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for archivfrdasstu130brauuoft.
evidence.summary: Identifies the University of Toronto/Robarts complete-volume scan and records possible-copyright-status NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, MS. Rawl. D. 82, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries.
evidence.locator: parent record; Part 1 items 1–2; Part 2; physical description and history
evidence.summary: Current official evidence for shelfmark, identifiers, fifteenth-century English origin, complete 34-leaf-plus-endleaves extent, binding, Part 1 hand and collation, inferred Thebes and Troy loci, titles, rubrics, incipits, explicits, and adjacent codex contents.

evidence.citation: Bodleian medieval-mss TEI source for MS. Rawl. D. 82.
evidence.summary: Auditable machine-readable source for the current catalog facts, including locus inference, the lack of foliation recorded for Part 1, and the 2025 catalog revision.

evidence.citation: Late Medieval English Scribes, Oxford, Bodleian Library MS Rawlinson D.82, manuscript ID 415.
evidence.summary: Describes the manuscript as two booklets with two scribes of similar date, dates it 1450–1500, localizes its language to Northamptonshire (LALME LP 6640), reports modern pencil foliation 1–34, and links sampled pages for hands 1 and 2.

evidence.citation: Late Medieval English Scribes full-page example for Hand 1, MS Rawlinson D.82, fol. 1r.
evidence.summary: Confirms one selected public color page view of the Thebes opening and states that images are all rights reserved, remain copyrighted by the holding libraries, and may not be used beyond private study without permission.

evidence.citation: Late Medieval English Scribes full-page example for Hand 2, MS Rawlinson D.82, fol. 25r.
evidence.summary: Confirms the second selected public color page view linked from the manuscript record. Fol. 25r has zero overlap with CME00076 and carries the same all-rights-reserved/private-study-only terms.

evidence.citation: Nathaniel E. Griffin, 'The Sege of Troye,' PMLA 22.1 (1907), 157–200, complete issue scan.
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the prior Troy edition, unique Rawlinson witness, Southern-dialect claim, folio ranges, narrative scope, source theory, and explicit editorial normalization and expansion practices.

evidence.citation: Cambridge Core, Nathaniel E. Griffin, 'V.—The Sege of Troye,' PMLA 22.1 (1907), DOI 10.2307/456664.
evidence.summary: Current bibliographic landing page and extract confirming article pages, anonymous fifteenth-century prose, unique Rawlinson witness, Southern-dialect assessment, and Argonautic-to-destruction narrative scope.

evidence.citation: Middle English Texts Series, The Siege of Thebes: Introduction.
evidence.summary: Current scholarly evidence for Lydgate's circa-1421–1422 verse Siege of Thebes and for the prose redaction in Rawl. D. 82 as a mid-fifteenth-century adaptation emphasizing the epic battle narrative.

evidence.citation: Middle English Texts Series, Troy Book: Selections, Introduction.
evidence.summary: States that Lydgate composed Troy Book from 1412 to 1420 and that it soon served as the source for a prose Sege of Troy retelling the story through the city's fall at the end of Book 4.

evidence.citation: Direct Digital Bodleian and IIIF checks for MS. Rawl. D. 82 catalog ARKs.
evidence.summary: The tested item and manifest routes returned 404 and the current catalog TEI has no digital-facsimile link. The only verified manuscript images are the separate LMES sample leaves at fols. 1r and 25r; only fol. 1r overlaps CME00076.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Special collections mediated copying.
evidence.summary: States that the service is available to all, supplies reference-quality PDF scans for transcription and analysis, ordinarily charges £1 per scan to non-Oxford users, assesses suitability case by case, and accepts orders by PDF form and email.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries Mediated Copying order form.
evidence.summary: Records £1 digital scans, £10 minimum, required shelfmark and recto/verso locators, PDF file-transfer delivery, private-research declarations, and CC BY-NC 4.0 terms for supplied scans of out-of-copyright material.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Order images from Imaging Services.
evidence.summary: Provides the high-resolution color TIFF route, posted first and subsequent image fees, large-order negotiation, technical specifications, and separate reproduction guidance.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries Imaging Services order form.
evidence.summary: Records £27 for the first color TIFF, £7.50 for images 2–99, £6.15 for later images, £20 minimum, required exact locators, and the CC BY-NC statement for out-of-copyright material.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries Imaging Services Terms of Use.
evidence.summary: Distinguishes permitted non-commercial and academic use from commercial reuse requiring permission and fees, defines commercial purposes, gives academic-publication guidance, and limits electronic permissions to their licensed term.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Bodleian Archives & Manuscripts Requests.
evidence.summary: Explains registration and the online request service for manuscripts to be viewed in Weston Library reading rooms, subject to item-level restrictions.

evidence.citation: Deutsche Digitale Bibliothek, Friedrich Brie, GND 116507896.
evidence.summary: Confirms Friedrich Brie's full life dates, occupation as Anglist, and Freiburg death in 1948.

open_questions.question: Will the Bodleian supply an authorized complete color surrogate of MS. Rawl. D. 82 rather than only the 24 leaves containing the corpus works?
next_steps: Request a quote for covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, three front and three rear later endleaves, every recto and verso of leaves 1–34 including blanks, and edges when offered.
Map Thebes to the current catalog's inferred fols. 1r–10v and Troy to inferred fols. 11r–24v after delivery.
Ask whether any existing internal photography can be supplied instead of new capture and whether its coverage includes binding surfaces.

open_questions.question: Does Fritz Jung's working transcript survive, and did Brie check or alter it against the original manuscript before publication?
next_steps: Search Brie's papers and correspondence for the transcript and editorial annotations.
Compare a future complete manuscript surrogate against Brie's printed readings and the CME encoding.

open_questions.question: How should Brie's and Griffin's a/b folio references be crosswalked to the current catalog's inferred recto/verso loci when the current catalog says Part 1 has no foliation but LMES reports modern pencil numbers 1–34?
next_steps: Inspect a complete color surrogate or the manuscript in person for foliation marks.
Ask the Bodleian whether the 2025 'No foliation' statement means no historical foliation, no visible numbering, or an intentionally inferred modern locus sequence.

open_questions.question: May a newly supplied complete-codex facsimile be redistributed in the public Git repository or used in commercial or non-commercial print-on-demand outputs?
next_steps: Keep all delivered images Git-ignored until the exact delivery terms and any written permission are reviewed.
Ask the Bodleian separately about repository redistribution, academic quotation, non-commercial self-publishing, and commercial print-on-demand use.
Do not treat CC BY-NC as permission for commercial outputs or let the public-domain status of the medieval text override image terms.

open_questions.question: Can the two prose adaptations be localized or dated more precisely without transferring the copy's Northamptonshire dialect and fifteenth-century witness date to composition?
next_steps: Review the full LALME profile LP 6640 and modern linguistic work on both prose texts.
Distinguish composition, adaptation, copying, and present manuscript evidence in any narrower claim.
